name: genius-client-sdk
documentation:
# Genius Client SDK

Details for how to use this SDK can be found [on the website](https://verses.gitbook.io/genius-documentation/6fG4baTqAyhcZpeLcucL/genius-overview/python-sdk)

classes.name: genius_client_sdk.configuration.BaseAgentConfig

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.configuration.BaseAgentConfig.agent_port
documentation: Port number for the agent, defaulting to 3000 if not set in the environment

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.configuration.BaseAgentConfig.agent_hostname
documentation: Hostname for the agent, defaulting to 'localhost' if not set in the environment

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.configuration.BaseAgentConfig.agent_http_protocol
documentation: HTTP protocol for the agent, defaulting to 'http' if not set in the environment

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.configuration.BaseAgentConfig.agent_url
documentation: URL for the agent, constructed from the protocol, hostname, and port

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.configuration.BaseAgentConfig.build_version
documentation: Build version of the application

class_variables.name: genius_client_sdk.configuration.BaseAgentConfig.DEFAULT_AGENT_PORT
documentation: Default port number for the agent

class_variables.name: genius_client_sdk.configuration.BaseAgentConfig.DEFAULT_AGENT_HOSTNAME
documentation: Default hostname for the agent

class_variables.name: genius_client_sdk.configuration.BaseAgentConfig.DEFAULT_AGENT_HTTP_PROTOCOL
documentation: Default HTTP protocol for the agent

class_variables.name: genius_client_sdk.configuration.BaseAgentConfig.DEFAULT_BUILD_VERSION
documentation: Default build version of the application

class_variables.name: genius_client_sdk.configuration.BaseAgentConfig.AGENT_URL_FORMAT
documentation: Format string for the agent URL
Configuration class to hold configuration values used by genius client sdk.

functions.name: genius_client_sdk.utils.send_http_request
documentation:
Send an API request across a port for GET, PUT, and POST and a call. Optionally takes json_data
as input for put or post.

functions.name: genius_client_sdk.utils.send_csv_request
documentation: POST CSV data over an API request across a port using the import call.

functions.name: genius_client_sdk.utils.inference_payload
documentation:
Constructs the JSON payload for inference (default to PGMPY) based on an evidence input.

Parameters:
variable_id (str): The ID of the variable for which inference is to be performed.
evidence (dict): A dictionary containing evidence where keys are variable names and values are observed values.

Returns:
dict: A dictionary representing the JSON payload for the inference request.

functions.name: genius_client_sdk.utils.learning_payload
documentation:
Takes in either:
    1) A list of variables and a nested list of observations for each variable. Each element of the
       nested list is like a new "row" of observations.
    2) A CSV file path with variables as column headers (first row) and observations as subsequent
       rows.

    The input is then parsed to create the JSON payload in the appropriate format.
    The data are turned into a string with the following structure:

    "{var1, var2,...}
{obs1, obs2,...}
{obs1, obs2,...}
..."

    where observations are repeated for each row of observations available.
    Learning defaults to PGMPY.

    Parameters:
    variables (list): A list of variable names.
    observations (list): A nested list of observations corresponding to the variables.
    csv_path (str): Path to a CSV file containing variables and observations.

    Returns:
    dict: A dictionary representing the JSON payload for the learning request.

functions.name: genius_client_sdk.utils.control_payload
documentation:
Constructs the JSON payload for action selection (default to pymdp) based on an evidence input.

Parameters:
variables (Union[int, np.ndarray]): Observation to be used in the payload
policy_len (int): integer representing the length of the policy.

Returns:
dict: A dictionary representing the JSON payload for the control request.

functions.name: genius_client_sdk.utils.onehot
documentation:
Generates a one hot numpy array

 Parameters:
 size (int): size of the array
 position (int): position in the array for the 1 hot

 Returns:
np.ndarray[Any, np.dtype[np.floating[Any] | np.float64]]: a one hot numpy array of dimension "size" with a 1 at the specified "position" and zeros
 for all other elements of the array

functions.name: genius_client_sdk.utils.control_map
documentation:
Maps from the integer dummy encoding of an action to the action's string name

Example: If the controls=["left", "right"] and chosen_action=1 then this function returns
         "right".

Parameters:
controls list[str]     : A list of strings containing possible actions
chosen_action int: An integer encoding of the selected action

Returns:
str : The string representation of the action

functions.name: genius_client_sdk.utils.policy_map
documentation:
Maps from the integer dummy encoding of a policy in the policy space and the policy string

Example: If policy_space=[["left", "right"], ["right", "left"], ["up", "down"]] and
         chosen_policy=2 then this function returns ["up", "down"].

Parameters:
policy_space (list[list[str]]) : The space of possible policies pursued by the agent. Can be
                                 obtained by using get_policy_space().
chosen_policy (int)            : An integer encoding the chosen policy in the policy space.

Returns:
list[str] : A policy list which contains a sequence of actions as a string.

functions.name: genius_client_sdk.utils.get_policy_space
documentation:
Constructs the space of all policies possible for the agent to pursue.

Note that this function can be used for multiple observation modalities or state factor which
is why the inputs for n_states and n_actions are lists.

Parameters:
n_states (list[int])  : A list of the number of states for each state factor.
n_actions (list[int]) : A list of the number of actions for each state factor.
policy_len (int)      : Length of policy (i.e. number of actions).
actions (list[str])   : String representation of possible actions.

Returns:
list[list[list[str]]]: Either the string representation of the policy
                                             space or the dummy encoding of the policy space.

functions.name: genius_client_sdk.utils.plot_categorical
documentation:
Helper function to plot a categorical distribution as a bar plot.


Parameters:
x (list): A list of categories or x-axis values.
y (list): A list of values corresponding to each category in x.
xlabel (list, optional): A list of labels for the x-axis. Defaults to None.
ylabel (list, optional): A list of labels for the y-axis. Defaults to None.
title (str, optional): The title of the plot. Defaults to None.
color (str, optional): The color of the bars in the plot. Defaults to "dodgerblue".
rotation (int, optional): The rotation angle of the x-axis labels. Defaults to 0.

functions.name: genius_client_sdk.utils.flatten_nested_list
documentation:
Helper function that flattens a nested list.

Parameters:
x (list): A list of categories or x-axis values.

Returns:
list: A flattened list.

classes.name: genius_client_sdk.model.GeniusModel

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.model.GeniusModel.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the GeniusModel.

Parameters:
version (str): The version of the model, default is the build version from Constants.
json_path (str, optional): The path to the JSON file for the model. Defaults to None.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.model.GeniusModel._from_json
documentation:
Loads a JSON file to a dict and validates it.

Parameters:
json_path (str): The path to the JSON file.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.model.GeniusModel.add_variables
documentation:
Adds a list of variables to the JSON model dict.

Parameters:
name (str): The name of the variable.
values (list): The values for the variable.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.model.GeniusModel.add_variable
documentation:
Adds a variable to the JSON model dict.

Parameters:
name (str): The name of the variable.
values (list): The values for the variable.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.model.GeniusModel.add_factor
documentation:
Adds a factor to the JSON model. Note: The reason for separating the conditioned variable
from its parents is to be able to construct the variable list as:

[conditioning variable, parent_1, parent_2, ...]

This structure is assumed in the VFG format but not explicitly stated. By asking the user
to construct the variable and its parents in this way it avoids situations where the user
might input the variables in the wrong order.

In the case of marginal distributions the parents field would be left defaulting to None.

Parameters:
values (np.ndarray): The values for the factor.
target (str): The conditioned (target) variable. Defaults to None.
parents (list[str]): The conditioning (parent) variables.
role (str, optional): The role of the factor. Defaults to None.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.model.GeniusModel.validate
documentation:
Validates that a model is a valid factor graph.

Parameters:
verbose (bool): If True, prints a success message upon validation. Defaults to True.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.model.GeniusModel.save
documentation:
Export JSON model dict to JSON file at a given path.

Parameters:
outpath (str): The path to save the JSON file.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.model.GeniusModel.visualize
documentation:
Visualize the JSON model dict.

Parameters:
factor_color (str): The color for the factor nodes. Defaults to "lightgreen".
variable_color (str): The color for the variable nodes. Defaults to "lightcoral".

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.model.GeniusModel._to_vfg
documentation:
Wrapper for vfg_from_json in pyvfg.

Parameters:
json_model (dict): The JSON model dict.

Returns:
The VFG object created from the JSON model.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.model.GeniusModel.get_variable_values
documentation:
Wrapper for pyvfg variable elements access.

Parameters:
variable_id (str): The ID of the variable.

Returns:
list: The values of the variable.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.model.GeniusModel.get_variable_names
documentation:
Wrapper for pyvfg variable keys access.

Returns:
list: The names of the variables.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.model.GeniusModel.get_factor_attributes
documentation:
Wrapper for pyvfg factor attributes access.

Parameters:
factor_id (int): The ID of the factor.
attribute (str): The attribute to retrieve.

Returns:
The value of the specified attribute.

Raises:
KeyError: If the attribute is not recognized.

The GeniusModel class is used to build factor graphs from scratch. The class has the following
capabilities:
- Create a model from a JSON file path
- Construct model by adding variables or factors
- Validate a constructed model with POST /validate in the fastAPI
- Save (export) a model to JSON
- Visualize the model with networkx
- Get variable names and values for a given model
- Get factor attributes for a given model

Internally, the model is stored in the json_model instance variable and is just a dict that is
converted to and from JSON as needed.

In the future, many of the methods in this class will become part of pyvfg and this class will
likely become a wrapper around pyvfg.

classes.name: genius_client_sdk.agent.GeniusAgent

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.agent.GeniusAgent.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the GeniusAgent instance.

Attributes:
model (GeniusModel or None): The model loaded into the agent. Initially set to None.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.agent.GeniusAgent.load_model_from_json
documentation:
Load a JSON model from a path.

Parameters:
json_path (str): The path to the JSON file containing the model.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.agent.GeniusAgent.load_genius_model
documentation:
Load a model from the GeniusModel class.

Parameters:
model (GeniusModel): An instance of the GeniusModel class.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.agent.GeniusAgent.log_model
documentation:
Prints the JSON model to logs.
Optionally, summarizes the model in a (slightly) more readable format.

Parameters:
summary (bool): If True, prints a summarized view of the model. Defaults to False.
logging_level (int): The logging level to use. Defaults to DEFAULT_LOGGING_LEVEL.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.agent.GeniusAgent.infer
documentation:
Run inference based on input variable to infer and evidence (observed data).

Parameters:
variable_id (str): The ID of the variable to infer.
evidence (dict): A dictionary containing observed data.
verbose (bool): If True, prints out probabilities. Defaults to True.

Returns:
dict: A dictionary containing the inference result.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.agent.GeniusAgent.learn
documentation:
Run learning based on observations of variables. Accepts either a list of variables and
nested list of observations or a path to a CSV file.

Parameters:
variables (list): A list of variables to learn.
observations (list): A nested list of observations for the variables.
csv_path (str): The path to a CSV file containing the observations.
verbose (bool): If True, prints out the JSON model file. Defaults to False.

Returns:
dict: A dictionary containing the learning result.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.agent.GeniusAgent.act
documentation:
Performs action selection given an observation array and policy length.

Parameters:
observation (np.ndarray): An array containing the observation data.
policy_len (int): The length of the policy. Defaults to 2.
verbose (bool): If True, prints out the action, inferred state, and policy distribution. Defaults to True.

Returns:
dict: A dictionary containing the action selection result.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.agent.GeniusAgent.structure_learning
documentation:
Performs structure learning given a CSV file. Structure learning entails taking a dataset
and attempting to build the dependencies in a probabilistic model from it. The resulting
model is then sent to the connected Genius agent.

If the learning argument is true, GeniusAgent.learn() will also be called to fill in the
probabilities.

Accepts either a list of variables and nested list of observations or a path to a CSV
file.

The GeniusAgent class is used as a wrapper around fastAPI to communicate to and from the Genius
agent. This class has the following capabilities enabled by the API calls:
- POST /graph of genius model loaded from a JSON or from the GeniusModel class
- GET /graph of genius model and print/view its contents
- POST /infer to perform inference given some evidence and a variable of interest
- POST /learn to perform parameter learning given an input CSV or list of variables and their
  observations
- POST /actionselection to perform action selection given a POMDP model structure and
  observation vector
- POST /import to perform structure learning given a CSV data file

At the moment, it is assumed that the user connects to a local GeniusAgent. In the future,
initializing this class will have options to specify a URL and port.

classes.name: genius_client_sdk.pomdp.POMDPModel

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.pomdp.POMDPModel.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the POMDPModel.

Parameters:
version (str): The version of the model, default is the build version from Constants.
json_path (str, optional): The path to the JSON file for the model. Defaults to None.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.pomdp.POMDPModel.add_likelihood_factor
documentation:
Initializes the POMDPModel.

Parameters:
version (str): The version of the model, default is the build version from Constants.
json_path (str, optional): The path to the JSON file for the model. Defaults to None.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.pomdp.POMDPModel.add_transition_factor
documentation:
Adds a transition factor to the model.

Parameters:
variables (list): The list of variables for the factor.
values (np.ndarray): The values for the factor.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.pomdp.POMDPModel.add_prior_factor
documentation:
Adds a prior factor to the model.

Parameters:
variables (list): The list of variables for the factor.
values (np.ndarray): The values for the factor.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.pomdp.POMDPModel.add_preference_factor
documentation:
Adds a preference factor to the model.

Parameters:
variables (list): The list of variables for the factor.
values (np.ndarray): The values for the factor.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.pomdp.POMDPModel.add_state_variable
documentation:
Adds a state variable to the model.

Parameters:
name (str): The name of the state variable.
values (list): The values for the state variable.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.pomdp.POMDPModel.add_observation_variable
documentation:
Adds an observation variable to the model.

Parameters:
name (str): The name of the observation variable.
values (list): The values for the observation variable.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.pomdp.POMDPModel.add_action_variable
documentation:
Adds an action variable to the model.

Parameters:
name (str): The name of the action variable.
values (list): The values for the action variable.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.pomdp.POMDPModel._check_flags
documentation:
Runs through each flag and determines which are false.

Raises:
Exception: If any required components are missing from the POMDP model.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.pomdp.POMDPModel._toggle_flag
documentation:
Toggles a flag from false to true.

Parameters:
component (str): The component whose flag is to be toggled.

methods.name: genius_client_sdk.pomdp.POMDPModel.validate
documentation:
Method override for parent class that just adds the _check_flags() statement to ensure all
model components are present before validation.

Parameters:
verbose (bool): If True, prints a success message upon validation. Defaults to True.

Creates a POMDP style factor graph model. This class is really just a wrapper around the
GeniusModel class with constrained functionality to enable the user to create a POMDP
model. Strictly speaking, one can create it with the GeniusModel class but the convenience
functions in this class make the process easier and include checks to make sure all the
necessary model components are present.

A POMDP model requires preset factors (with specific roles) and specific variables. The initial
state prior is optional. Each time a component is added, the corresponding flags dict is toggled to true.
